verdict_reasoning: TGN46/TGN38 is a type I single-pass TM protein predominantly localized to the trans-Golgi network, but it cycles continuously through the plasma membrane as part of its sorting itinerary (TGN → PM → endosomes → TGN). Surface biotinylation and anti-TGN46 antibody uptake assays on intact cells reliably detect a minority PM pool, and this cycling is exploited experimentally for endocytic-uptake studies. The TM precursor also undergoes regulated ectodomain shedding. Dominant compartment is TGN/endosomal, but a documented transient PM pool with its own TM anchor qualifies as dual_localization. Not yes — steady-state surface fraction is small and the protein is canonically a TGN resident.
